steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The task is to connect Sherman’s quotation — about maps presenting ‘a powerful visual representation of the transformation of Manchester’s landscape’ — to the increase in the UK’s urban population during the Industrial Revolution. The answer must accurately use both pieces and show how the maps link the two.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘the UK experienced a substantial (2,000%) increase in population’: The 2,000% figure applies to Manchester specifically, not the UK overall. The UK figure is 30%. This misattributes a statistic and creates a factual error. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Sherman describes [relocation] as “a powerful visual representation”’: Sherman’s quote describes maps, not the act of rural populations relocating. This distorts what Sherman actually said. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘period maps of the UK present “a powerful visual representation”’: Sherman’s quote refers specifically to maps of Manchester, not maps of the UK broadly. This overgeneralizes her claim. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘The sizeable growth in the UK’s urban population … is reflected in before-and-after maps that present, in Sherman’s view, “a powerful visual representation” of how the increase transformed Manchester’: Opens with the UK urban population growth (the stat to connect), links it to the before-and-after maps of Manchester, and incorporates Sherman’s exact quote about what those maps show — accurately and completely accomplishing the task. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
